general_guidelines: Drink water throughout the day, even if you do not feel thirsty.
Avoid sugary, caffeinated, or alcoholic beverages, as they can accelerate dehydration.
Keep water close at hand in a thermos or insulated bottle.

demographic_adjustments.children: Ensure children drink small amounts of water frequently. Offer water-rich fruits like watermelon or oranges.
demographic_adjustments.elderly: The sensation of thirst decreases with age. Caregivers should prompt elderly individuals to drink water hourly.
demographic_adjustments.outdoor_workers: Outdoor workers should drink water or electrolyte solutions (like ORS) every 15-20 minutes during extreme heat.
demographic_adjustments.pregnant_lactating: Increase baseline water intake by 0.5 to 1.0 liters per day.

electrolytes_rehydration.when_needed: During intense activity lasting more than 1 hour, or under extreme heat index levels.

solutions.name: ORS (Oral Rehydration Salts)
solutions.recipe: Dissolve 1 packet of WHO-approved ORS in 1 liter of clean water.

solutions.name: Homemade Rehydration Solution
solutions.recipe: Mix 6 teaspoons of sugar and 0.5 teaspoon of salt in 1 liter of clean water.
